name: Gas exchange half-saturation surface area
tier_rationale: Tier C: morphometric model parameter from Mayhew 1986 Placenta (PMC-verified): the term-placenta morphometric diffusing capacity depends most strongly on harmonic-mean membrane thickness and villous surface area. The half-saturation area is a model-fit abstraction, not directly reported.
notes: Villous surface area at which placental gas-equilibration efficiency reaches half of its asymptote. Caveats: Phenomenological model parameter mapping morphometric diffusing capacity onto a saturable equilibrator. Validate against UV PO2 endpoint.

name: Maximum venous-equilibrator fraction
tier_rationale: Tier C from Carter & Pijnenborg 2011 BPOBG: review of invasive placentation in primates including human. Maximum equilibration (fraction of P_arterial - P_venous that the placenta can clear) is a model abstraction informed by morphometric and Doppler-flow constraints.
notes: Asymptotic upper bound on placental gas-equilibration efficiency as villous area increases. Caveats: Saturation cap derived to reproduce the textbook 25-40 mmHg UV PO2 window at term.
